titleStmt.title: The Chargé in Finland ( (McClintock) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Chargé in Finland ( McClintock ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 1071. During my recent trip to Stockholm the Jugoslav ChargéNicola Naditch, Yugoslav Counselor of Legation and Chargé in Sweden. told me he had recently had a long conversation with Soviet Chargé, Semenov,Vladimir Semenovich Semenov, Soviet Chargé in Sweden. on question of possible peace terms for Finland. Russian Chargé quoted an alleged statement of Peter the Great to effect that “Viborg is the cushion of Petersburg”In a peace settlement concluded with Sweden at Nystad (Uusikaupunki), Finland, on August 31, 1721, Tsar Peter the Great acquired for Russia the province of Vyborg (Viipuri) as a protective strip to the northern approaches of St. Petersburg, and other territory in Karelia. and said that he thought Soviet Government would insist on retention of Viborg as a condition of peace with Finland but might be willing to give up Hangö. This is the first time I have heard of any Russian disposition to relinquish this port but such an intention, if true, would go far to allay opinion in Sweden and might make it difficult for a Finnish Government to refuse a peace embodying the retention of Hangö by the Finns. On other hand loss of Viborg as suggested in my 942, July 28Not printed. would gravely cripple economy of eastern Finland and as indicated in my 305, February 18Not printed. would have severe domestic repercussions following [compelling?] another evacuation of the 450,000 Karelians and their forced quartering on the rest of the Finnish population.
Repeated to Stockholm as my 280, today.
